commandTemplate: ONEAPI_DEVICE_SELECTOR=level_zero:0,1,2,3 GGML_SYCL_DISABLE_DNN=1 llama-bench -v -m <model> -dev SYCL0/SYCL1/SYCL2/SYCL3 -ngl 99 -sm row -ts 1/1/1/1 -ncmoe <N> -p 0 -n 1 -b 128 -ub 32 -fa 0 -ctk f16 -ctv f16 -t 8 -r 1 -o jsonl -oe jsonl
interpretation: Each additional ncmoe block moves the first failed GPU expert allocation roughly 12 layers later.
The failures are small 129761280 byte split expert allocations after cumulative GPU-resident expert pressure, not one malformed tensor.
The current row-split path can only keep about 12-13 expert layers per B70 before split-buffer allocation fails.
ncmoe=62 can load because the experts are effectively CPU/file-backed, but that is not a useful performance mode on this low-RAM host.
Do not submit MiniMax performance to LocalMaxxing until GPU-resident expert execution works.
nextActions: Stop treating MiniMax as flag tuning.
Implement or prototype SYCL split-buffer allocation behavior suitable for expert tensors.
Implement expert-aligned GGML_OP_MUL_MAT_ID execution for SYCL split buffers, or keep selected experts on the owning B70 and assemble outputs correctly.
